---
title: "Report Preparation Guide"
---

## Basics

This document serves as an aide memoire to guide the production of the BRA-EUR report.

:::{.callout-tip}
## General design criteria

* the report is built using quarto and the R-ecosystem, the final output in form of a pdf version and accompanying html (quarto) book
* assciated chapters are managed / included via _quarto.yml, supporting and releasable documents are provided (e.g. this aide-memoire)
* reserved folders
  + figures -- keeps all embedded figures (e.g. maps), if required "tailored" figures may be composed using powerpoint (and save as): __figures-handmade.pptx
  + R -- all R functions (once steady and industrialised)
  + scripts -- temporary, report-specific ingestion/build scripts that are needed for reproducibility but are not yet part of the PBWG package
  + data -- the data to reproduce (and render) the report, ideally in (harmonised) PBWG format
  + docs -- reserved, this folder is used for the hosting of the html report on github
  + (gitignored) _book-local -- to support local rendering of contributors
:::

This report is produced by various individuals with different programming backgrounds.
The following coding style shall serve to increase the readability of code and its review/troubleshooting.    
Some of the work may be outsourced to another project aiming at producing a supporting R-package.

:::{.callout-note}
## Coding principles

* the principle paradigm is i.) read (analytic or performance-related) data, ii.) process and prepare the data for the analysis at hand, and iii.) "communicate" (either as table or visualisation) the results
* these steps shall be performed starting from the agreed harmonised "PBWG" format or a proposed version (if trialled first in BRA-EUR)
* associated functions shall be **generic**, i.e., not building on specifics of the regional data
* specific folders -- c.f. above
* the report and supporting repo will be "public" - use .gitignore 
* gitignore "large" source data/files to avoid issues with github,      
  share the files via joint folders/file transfer
* coding style
  + variables and function-names: lower-case with underscore, e.g. asmas, calc_travel_time     
    try to name what it is or does, e.g. taxi-in times: txits, ggplot function: plot_fancy_thing
  + dataframe/tibble: column-names upper-case for all standard aviation terms, e.g. actual taxi-in time: TXIT, use underscore prefix/postfix, e.g. TOT_TXIT
  + quarto tags: use hyphen, e.g. fig-cool-plot, sec-herehappenssomething
  + file names: use hyphen
  
:::


## Overall Data Flow/Process

Both groups process and expose their data in different systems and technologies.     
While an open exchange of the underlying **source data** is a means to continue with the harmonisation of the data processes, the goal is to arrive at a set of harmonised (PBWG) data sets for the common indicators.    
Accordingly, the source data extraction may differ between the parties, e.g. Brazil/DECEA is developing endpoints for API queries, ECTRL/PRU is limited by the on-going cloud transition and currently access their data via a classical data warehouse/SQL.

The BRA-EUR report builds on the PBWG work and will propose changes to the latter during further developments.

```{r}
#| out-width: 100%
knitr::include_graphics("./figures/data-source-analytic-perfrelated.png")
```

The report broadly distinguishes between network-level and airport-level reporting.

## Network-level Data

Current temporary ingestion/build scripts are kept in `scripts/`.
They are report-specific and should remain outside the PBWG package until the data flow is stable enough to generalise.

* `scripts/build-bra-network-traffic-from-totalbr-temporary.R` prepares the Brazil network traffic series from the TotalBR extract.
  It writes the harmonised APDF-like movement extract and PBWG network traffic CSVs to `../PBWG-BRA/data/network-traffic` and `../PBWG-BRA/outputs/network-traffic`, then copies the report-facing combined file to `data/PBWG-BRA-network-traffic-2023-2025.csv`.
  The script treats the official Brazil indicator prefixes (`SB`, `SD`, `SI`, `SJ`, `SN`, `SS`, `SW`) and numeric/non-standard TotalBR indicators as regional, and applies the temporary `AFIL` handling described in the traffic characterisation notes.
* `scripts/build-network-flow-summary-temporary.R` prepares the Chapter 3 network-flow canonical form for the airport-rank curve, DAIO share, Brazil-Europe flow view, and destination-region charts.
  The core output is `data/PBWG-BRA-EUR-network-flow-pairs-2025.csv`, with one row per reporting region, year, airport pair, DAIO class, origin/destination country, origin/destination world region, and flight count.
  This form should be straightforward for other benchmarking parties to reproduce: provide `YEAR`, `ADEP`, `ADES`, `FLTS`, a local regional-airport classifier, and an airport/country/region lookup.
  The derived report-facing summaries are kept in `data/PBWG-BRA-EUR-airport-departure-rank-2025.csv`, `data/PBWG-BRA-EUR-daio-share-2025.csv`, `data/PBWG-BRA-EUR-world-region-departures-2025.csv`, and `data/PBWG-BRA-EUR-country-departures-2025.csv`.
  Placeholder destinations such as `ZZZZ` are retained as `Unmapped` in the canonical product, but excluded from mapped world-region shares in the report figures.
* `scripts/build-bra-apt-tfc-2023-2025-temporary.R` prepares the temporary Brazil airport traffic file used by Chapter 3 until the upstream APDF airport-traffic pipeline provides the standard dataset.
* These scripts should be revisited once Brazil APDF/network outputs are available from the regular PBWG workflow.
  At that point the report should consume the standard PBWG outputs directly and these temporary scripts can either be retired or converted into documentation-only provenance records.

Current data placement:

* Raw TotalBR input is kept outside git at `../PBWG-BRA/data-src/raw/totalbr.parquet`.
  This file is large and should not be committed to GitHub.
* Brazil reusable network outputs are kept under `../PBWG-BRA/outputs/network-traffic/`.
  The APDF-like TotalBR coercion output is kept under `../PBWG-BRA/data/network-traffic/`.
* Europe reusable network outputs are kept under `../PBWG-data/network-traffic/`.
* The reusable cross-region canonical flow-pair file is mirrored under `../PBWG-BRA/outputs/network-flow/` and `../PBWG-data/network-flow/`.
* Report-facing analytical cuts are committed in this repository under `data/` so the chapter can be rendered reproducibly without rebuilding the heavy raw extracts.
  These include the airport departure rank, DAIO share, external world-region, and country-level summaries derived from the canonical flow-pair file.
* The Chapter 3 airport movement visualisations are intentionally scoped to the 12 study airports in each region.
  A future report may expand this study panel to the top 15 or 20 airports if omitted airports have material performance relevance.

## Airport-level Data

### APDF - Airport Data Flow

The APDF format represents a compact airport-level format from which the common GANP indicators can be calculated, including variants.   
The ADPF data sets ensure also a decent basis for the on-going developments.

TODO - describe format
